tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all fail-to-pass tests are pure text-matching: they read the .cpp file and assert on specific strings like 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string', 'insertFromItself', '1\'000\'000', etc. The only execution-based check is a syntax check via clang that allows many errors to pass. No test actually compiles and runs the code to verify the buffer overflow is fixed.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub implementation that simply text-replaces the specific strings the tests look for (e.g., adding 'PaddedPODArray<UInt8> pad_string' and 'insertFromItself' as comments) could pass the tests without actually fixing the heap-buffer-overflow bug. The tests don't execute the code.
